---
title: "Literature Review"
---

## Research Landscape

- **Foundational work**: [2–3 seminal papers] — established the core formulation
- **Recent advances**: [3–5 papers from last 3 years] — state of the field
- **Adjacent areas**: [methods from related tasks that could transfer]
- **Negative results**: any published failures that bound what works

```{dot}
//| label: fig-landscape
//| fig-cap: "Literature landscape: foundations → approaches → this work"
//| fig-width: 7
//| fig-height: 3.5
digraph G {
  bgcolor="transparent"
  rankdir=TB
  graph [ranksep=0.55, nodesep=0.5]
  node [style=filled, fillcolor="#2c3035", color="#4a9eda",
        fontcolor="#f8f9fa", fontname="Arial", fontsize=11,
        shape=box, width=1.6, height=0.42, margin="0.15,0.08"]
  edge [color="#6c757d", penwidth=1.4]

  subgraph row1 {
    rank=same
    F1 [label="Foundation A", fillcolor="#375a7f"]
    F2 [label="Foundation B", fillcolor="#375a7f"]
    F3 [label="Foundation C", fillcolor="#375a7f"]
  }
  subgraph row2 {
    rank=same
    A1 [label="Approach X"]
    A2 [label="Approach Y"]
    A3 [label="Approach Z"]
  }
  subgraph row3 {
    rank=same
    G1 [label="Gap 1", fillcolor="#4a2040", color="#e74c3c"]
    G2 [label="Gap 2", fillcolor="#4a2040", color="#e74c3c"]
    G3 [label="Gap 3", fillcolor="#4a2040", color="#e74c3c"]
  }
  subgraph row4 {
    rank=same
    Us [label="This Work", fillcolor="#1a4a3a", color="#00bc8c", width=4.8]
  }

  F1 -> A1  F2 -> A2  F3 -> A3
  A1 -> G1  A2 -> G2  A3 -> G3
  G1 -> Us  G2 -> Us  G3 -> Us
}
```

---

## Key Papers

For each paper, fill in: **what it proposes**, **why it matters**, and **its limitation**
relative to this work. Use citation keys from `references.bib`.

- **[@paper2020a]** — [one-line summary]. Limitation: [gap this work addresses]
- **[@paper2021b]** — [one-line summary]. Limitation: [gap this work addresses]
- **[@paper2022c]** — [one-line summary]. Limitation: [gap this work addresses]
- **[@paper2023d]** — [one-line summary]. Limitation: [gap this work addresses]
- **[@paper2024e]** — [one-line summary]. Limitation: [gap this work addresses]

```{dot}
//| label: fig-papers
//| fig-cap: "Paper dependency graph (arrows = builds-on)"
//| fig-width: 7
//| fig-height: 3.2
digraph G {
  bgcolor="transparent"
  rankdir=LR
  graph [ranksep=0.6, nodesep=0.4]
  node [style=filled, fillcolor="#2c3035", color="#4a9eda",
        fontcolor="#f8f9fa", fontname="Arial", fontsize=11,
        shape=box, width=1.55, height=0.42]
  edge [color="#6c757d", penwidth=1.4]

  subgraph col1 {
    rank=same
    P1 [label="[Ref A]", fillcolor="#375a7f"]
    P2 [label="[Ref B]", fillcolor="#375a7f"]
  }
  subgraph col2 {
    rank=same
    P3 [label="[Ref C]"]
    P4 [label="[Ref D]"]
  }
  subgraph col3 {
    rank=same
    Us [label="This Work", fillcolor="#1a4a3a", color="#00bc8c"]
  }

  P1 -> P3  P1 -> P4
  P2 -> P3  P2 -> P4
  P3 -> Us  P4 -> Us
}
```

---

## Gap Analysis

- **Methodological gap**: existing methods fail at [specific scenario] — motivates the core technique
- **Empirical gap**: no systematic study of [factor X] on [dataset type] — motivates ablation design
- **Scalability gap**: prior work evaluated only at [scale] — motivates efficiency experiments

```{dot}
//| label: fig-gaps
//| fig-cap: "Gap → contribution mapping"
//| fig-width: 6.5
//| fig-height: 2.8
digraph G {
  bgcolor="transparent"
  rankdir=LR
  graph [ranksep=0.6, nodesep=0.45]
  node [style=filled, fillcolor="#2c3035", color="#4a9eda",
        fontcolor="#f8f9fa", fontname="Arial", fontsize=11,
        shape=box, width=1.55, height=0.42]
  edge [color="#6c757d", penwidth=1.4]

  subgraph col1 {
    rank=same
    G1 [label="Method Gap", fillcolor="#4a2040", color="#e74c3c"]
    G2 [label="Empirical Gap", fillcolor="#4a2040", color="#e74c3c"]
    G3 [label="Scale Gap", fillcolor="#4a2040", color="#e74c3c"]
  }
  subgraph col2 {
    rank=same
    C1 [label="Contribution 1", fillcolor="#1a4a3a", color="#00bc8c"]
    C2 [label="Contribution 2", fillcolor="#1a4a3a", color="#00bc8c"]
    C3 [label="Contribution 3", fillcolor="#1a4a3a", color="#00bc8c"]
  }

  G1 -> C1  G2 -> C2  G3 -> C3
}
```

---

## Positioning

- **vs. [Method A]**: same problem, different mechanism — we outperform by [margin] on [metric]
- **vs. [Method B]**: different problem formulation — ours is more general because [reason]
- **vs. [Method C]**: concurrent work — key difference is [design choice X]

```{dot}
//| label: fig-position
//| fig-cap: "Positioning relative to closest competitors"
//| fig-width: 6.5
//| fig-height: 2.4
digraph G {
  bgcolor="transparent"
  rankdir=TB
  graph [ranksep=0.5, nodesep=0.55]
  node [style=filled, fillcolor="#2c3035", color="#4a9eda",
        fontcolor="#f8f9fa", fontname="Arial", fontsize=11,
        shape=box, width=1.55, height=0.42]
  edge [color="#6c757d", penwidth=1.4]

  subgraph row1 {
    rank=same
    MA [label="Method A"]
    MB [label="Method B"]
    MC [label="Method C"]
  }
  subgraph row2 {
    rank=same
    Us [label="This Work", fillcolor="#1a4a3a", color="#00bc8c", width=5.0]
  }

  MA -> Us [label="  generalises", fontcolor="#adb5bd", fontsize=9]
  MB -> Us [label="  extends", fontcolor="#adb5bd", fontsize=9]
  MC -> Us [label="  improves", fontcolor="#adb5bd", fontsize=9]
}
```
